titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Marjorie Welish Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff and Emily Komornik

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 7480734.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Marjorie Welish papers document the life and work of poet, artist, and art critic Marjorie Welish and consist of professional correspondence, drafts of writings, notebooks, research files, course material, teaching notes, printed material, computer media, and audiovisual materials, 1950 to 2019.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2006, and the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2019.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 18 and 44 (computer media): Restricted Fragile Material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 34 (student records): Restricted until 2107. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 45 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Marjorie Welish Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Marjorie Welish Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection includes materials previously identified by the following call number: Uncat MSS 867.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, and competing priorities. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization at the time of acquisition. Further organization and description were carried out in 2011. As a rule, descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section is drawn in large part from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing, indicated by brackets.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Marjorie Welish (1944-)
bioghist.p: Marjorie Welish is an American poet, artist, and art critic who has held teaching appointments at institutions including Brown University, Cambridge University, and the Pratt Institute. She received a B.A. from Columbia University and an M.F.A. from Vermont College and Norwich University. Welish has received various fellowships and awards, including a Senior Fulbright Fellowship, a residency at the School of the Art Institute of Chicago, a Gottlieb Foundation Grant, and a Guggenheim Foundation Fellowship. Her art has been exhibited in New York, Paris, Vienna, and Cambridge. Welish is currently the Madelon Leventhal Rand Endowed Chair in Literature at Brooklyn College.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Marjorie Welish Papers document the life and work of poet, artist, and art critic Marjorie Welish and consist of professional correspondence, drafts of writings, notebooks, research files, course material, teaching notes, printed material, computer media, and audiovisual materials, 1950 to 2019.

The bulk of the collection consists of Welish's correspondence and drafts of writings. Writings shed light on her creative process and include autograph manuscript and typescript drafts and printed versions of her writings, including her poetry, essays, and criticism. Correspondence includes letters from colleagues, students, and friends regarding her teaching and writings. Other papers include research notes, her notes from courses she enrolled in, her teaching notes and class material, and various printed material that she collected as part of her research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Correspondence, 1950-2003. II. Writings, 1950-2003. III. Other Papers, 1966-1999. IV. Printed Material, 1970-2003. V. Computer media, undated. VI. December 2020 Acquisition, 1959-2019 and undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence includes letters from colleagues, students, and friends regarding her teaching and writings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Not arranged in any particular order.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under the headings of poetry, criticism, and Welish's collection of essays, "Signifying Art: Essays on Art after 1960."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains drafts of poetry and criticism by Welish. Included in the files are printed materials and research notes collected by Welish during her process of writing criticism.

"Signifying Art," proofs and "Narrating the Hand," manuscript
"The Sketch as Theory," final manuscript pages for chapters 14 and 20 and introduction
Pratt notes, notes on "Minimalism and Excellence," AICA board notes, correspondence
"When Is A Door Not A Door?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains research notes, notes from courses Welish took, teaching notes, and class material.

did.unittitle: African art and music class notes and sketches
Primitive art class notes and sketches
Art History class notes and stamp collection

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Not arranged in any particular order.

did.unittitle: The Academy of American Poets award ceremony

accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile material. Reference copies available. Consult access services for futher information.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2020.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, professional works, including art and writings, teaching materials, personal papers, computer media, and audiovisual materials by or relating to Marjorie Welish, 1959 to 2019.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five subseries: Correspondence, Writings and Art, Teaching Material, Personal Papers, and Computer and Audiovisual Media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 34 (student records): Restricted until 2107. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 44 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 45 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, including printed emails, from fellow artists and poets, curators, publishers, editors, students, and colleagues. Notable correspondents include poet Andrew Crozier, writer Terrence Diggory, writer Forrest Gander, poet Carla Harryman, poet Stephanie Strickland, and poet John Taggart. Correspondence discusses Welish's work, teaching appointments, exhibitions, guest lectures, and conference presentations.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically and sub-arranged alphabetically by correspondent name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials related to Welish's work as an author and artist, including drafts, proofs, and printed versions of her poetry, books, and art criticism; and files for solo and collaborative art projects, as well as sketches, drawings, paintings, and collages. Other professional papers include publicity materials and publishing correspondence, exhibition documentation, and grant, fellowship, and residency applications. Other writings contain class notes taken by Welish as an undergraduate at Columbia University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five groupings: Artwork and Art Projects, Books and Poetry, Art Criticism, Professional Papers, and Other Writings. Materials within this series maintain the original physical location found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Photocopied notes for poetry and paintings

title.part: The Studio Visit and Other Stories

"The Application Series:" research, writing, and some art sketches

The First Five Years edited typescript

Welish's reviews of poetry, books, plays, literary works

title.part: Signifying Art: Essays on Art Since 1960

did.unittitle: Writing by others on MW's art criticism
"When is a Door Not a Door?"
[Binder of collected news clippings and criticism, Marjorie Welish]
Manhattan East articles by Marjorie Welish
MW poems in magazines and anthologies
MW as juror [nominating and judging poetry prizes]
Writing on MW's poetry by others [reviews]
Fullbright/Council for International Exchange of Scholars (CIES)
Elizabeth Foundation of the Arts and International Studio and Curatorial Program
School of the Art Institute of Chicago
Residencies: Yaddo, Djerassi, MacDowell, and others
Creative Artists Public Service and New York Foundation for the Arts
American Academy of Arts and Letters (AAAL)
Catalogue of American Society of Contemporary Artists and International Association of Art Critics
The New Modern American and British Poetry [annotated]
Postcards with images of Vermont College students

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains papers related to Welish's teaching appointments at institutions such as Brooklyn College, Brown University, Columbia University, and Edinburgh College of Art. Included are syllabi, course notes, photocopied and annotated articles, exams, assignments, and administrative correspondence regarding seminars and undergraduate and graduate courses. Welish taught English literature and poetry; and art history, theory, and criticism. Student evaluations of Welish's classes, as well as Welish's evaluations of student work, are present. There is also a large volume of clippings Welish used as a resource in teaching topics such as art, literature, color theory, poetry, and creative writing.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into 11 groupings: General Reference Files, Brooklyn College, Pratt Institute, Columbia University, Brown University, School of Visual Arts, Goethe University Frankfurt, University of Cambridge, St. Edmund's College, Edinburgh College of Art, and The New School. Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 34 (student records): Restricted until 2107. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

When Did Modernity Begin?, primary and secondary sources

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 34 (student records): Restricted until 2107. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

Frankfurt [American Studies: Becoming Modern in Art and Art Criticism]
Re-reading Objectivism, Judith E. Wilson fellowship lectures, notes
Re-reading Objectivism, course lecture notes, primary sources
Judith E. Wilson fellowship lecture, week six

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains memorabilia files, including photographs, clippings, postcards, flyers, brochures, playbills, and announcements for exhibitions, lectures, and events Welish attended. Also present are folders containing memorabilia from Welish's travels to countries such as Germany, France, Austria, Hungary, the Netherlands, Ireland, the Czech Republic, Italy, and the United Kingdom. A portion of materials, including photographs, news clippings, and correspondence, relates to Welish's mother, Hester Welish, who was also an artist.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: Memorabilia and Hester Welish. Materials within this subseries maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five groupings: Visual Arts, Performing Arts, Literature, Personal Memorabilia, and Travel.

did.unittitle: Photographs, Marjorie Welish's apartment and bookshelves
[Solo and group exhibition announcement cards, press releases]
[Binder of collected news clippings and exhibitions, Hester Welish]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains compact discs, floppy disks, and audiocassettes with recordings of lectures and readings, interviews, and draft works; and three hard drives, including over 4,500 emails which document collaborations, exhibitions, relationships, projects, and teaching.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings by format: Computer Media and Audiovisual Media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 44 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 45 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
